g.data-narration: Welcome to this comprehensive lecture on the Ethereum Network, one of the most important blockchain platforms in the world.
g.text: A Comprehensive Guide to Decentralized Applications and Smart Contracts

g.data-narration: Ethereum is a decentralized, open-source blockchain platform that enables smart contracts and dApps. Founded by Vitalik Buterin in 2014.
g.text: A decentralized, open-source blockchain platform for creating

g.data-narration: Ethereum operates as a distributed ledger technology, where transactions are verified and recorded across a global network of nodes.
Ethereum clients are software programs enabling users to interact with the network. Key clients include Geth (Go), Parity (Rust), and PyEthereum (Python).
The Ethereum stack has four main layers: Application, Consensus, Data, and Network layers. Each serves a specific purpose in the ecosystem.
The Ethereum blockchain stores all transactions and data as a series of blocks. Each block contains a list of verified transactions.
Ethereum has two native cryptocurrencies: Ether (ETH) is the primary currency, while Ethereum Classic (ETC) is the original chain from the 2016 fork.
Forks are protocol changes. Hard forks are not backward compatible, while soft forks maintain compatibility with older versions.
Gas measures computational effort for transactions and smart contracts. Every operation requires gas, preventing spam and ensuring fair resource allocation.
g.text: Gas is the unit of measurement for computational work required to execute
transactions or smart contracts on the Ethereum network.

g.data-narration: Ethereum uses Proof-of-Work consensus. Nodes solve complex mathematical puzzles to validate transactions and create new blocks on the network.
The world state represents the current state of Ethereum - all accounts, balances, and smart contracts, updated with each new block.
g.text: The current state of Ethereum including all accounts, balances,
and smart contracts. Updated with each new block.

g.data-narration: Ethereum has two transaction types: Contract Creation transactions create new smart contracts, while Message Call transactions execute functions on existing contracts.
Key elements include Blocks (transaction collections), Transactions (single entries), Accounts (unique identifiers), and Smart Contracts (self-executing programs).
The Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM) is a Turing-complete virtual machine that executes smart contracts. It can run any program written in a Turing-complete language.
g.text: Turing-complete virtual machine for executing smart contracts
Can execute any program in a Turing-complete language

g.data-narration: In summary: Ethereum is a decentralized platform with smart contracts, powered by ETH, managed through gas fees, and secured by Proof-of-Work consensus.
g.text: • Decentralized blockchain platform for smart contracts & dApps
• Four-layer stack: Application, Consensus, Data, Network
• Gas measures computational effort for transactions

g.data-narration: For exams: Understand client types, stack layers, ETH vs ETC differences, gas concept, consensus mechanism, and world state updates.
g.text: 1. Review different Ethereum clients and their programming languages
2. Study the four layers of the Ethereum stack
3. Understand gas: Gas Limit × Gas Price = Fee
4. Know Proof-of-Work and how blocks are validated
